name: caprese salad
instructions: Toss mixed greens with tomato vinaigrette. Tear fresh mozzarella or paneer into chunks and fold in. Top with whole basil leaves (torn at the last moment, or left whole). Serve with bread on the side.
notes:
Modified caprese — uses tomato vinaigrette in place of sliced fresh tomatoes.

WHY VINAIGRETTE OVER SLICED TOMATO: a tomato vinaigrette (see the tomato vinaigrette recipe — samin nosrat's macerated-shallot-and-grated-tomato dressing) carries more concentrated tomato flavor than raw sliced tomato, and it dresses the greens evenly without the watery puddle that sliced tomato produces. The traditional caprese is sliced tomato + mozzarella + basil + olive oil, and that works, but the vinaigrette version reads cleaner and the flavor is more present.

CHEESE FORK: mozzarella or paneer — both work. Fresh mozzarella has the classic caprese-yielding texture; paneer is firmer and reads more substantial (and surprisingly, it works just fine here — the indian fresh cheese slot maps cleanly onto the italian fresh cheese slot for this salad).

BASIL: whole or torn leaves only. Chiffonade and chopping bruise the basil — see the herbs and greens article on basil handling. The flavor needs to be on the plate as fresh as possible.

BREAD: served alongside, not in the salad. The bread soaks up the tomato vinaigrette that pools on the plate — that's part of the eating experience, not a sandwich move.
